A limit order lets you set the price you're willing to accept — it waits in the market until someone matches it at that price or better, or it expires. A market order executes immediately against the best available price from users already offering.

Use a limit order when you're willing to wait for a specific price. Use a market order when you want to fill right now and don't mind taking the current best offer.

On Novig's peer-to-peer exchange, both work the same way — you're matched against other users, not a house — but the timing and certainty differ. A limit order might not fill if no one comes in at your price. A market order fills instantly but at whatever price is currently available.

If you want details on how Novig prioritizes orders or handles partial fills, a human can walk you through that.

Novig Support

The order book matches orders peer-to-peer — your order gets filled by another user's opposing order, not by a house. Orders commonly follow price-time priority (best price first, then earliest timestamp), but the exact matching rules and how simultaneous orders are handled are specifics I can't confirm.

If you're trying to understand how a particular order of yours was filled or ranked, send the order ID and we can have our ops team walk through what happened.
